title: A Sentimental Journey Through France and Italy
description: A Sentimental Journey Through France and Italy — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "A Sentimental Journey Through France and Italy" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: Table of Contents
memes.description: Full table of contents for A Sentimental Journey Through France and Italy

memes.label: Abdera
memes.description: A classical Thracian town invoked by Sterne in the allegorical fragment — the city transformed from vice to virtue by a performance of Euripides' Andromeda.

memes.label: Amiens
memes.description: The French town where Yorick receives a letter for Madame R— from the Count de L—'s sister — a crucial turning point that sets the Paris narrative in motion.

memes.label: Blush
memes.description: The blush as the body's involuntary moral signal in Sterne's novel — an ungovernable physical response that reveals character more reliably than words.

memes.label: Boulevards
memes.description: The Parisian boulevards — the fashionable tree-lined promenades where La Fleur courts the petite demoiselle of the Count de B—'s household.

memes.label: Boulogne
memes.description: A French port town on the Channel coast — referenced in Sterne's novel through the satirical figure of Smelfungus, who began his joyless tour there.

memes.label: Bourbonnois
memes.description: A historic province in central France — "the sweetest part of France" — through which Yorick travels during the vintage season, his anticipated joy shadowed by the memory of Maria.

memes.label: Brittany
memes.description: An ancient French province and duchy whose unique laws allowed deposed nobles to take up commerce without forfeiting their rank — the legal provision that preserves the Marquis d'E—'s honour in Sterne's novel.

memes.label: Brussels
memes.description: Capital of the Austrian Netherlands, home city of the fille de chambre, and the route junction Yorick refuses to pass through unless Eliza accompanies him.

memes.label: Calais
memes.description: The French port city where Yorick begins his sentimental journey — a threshold between nations, identities, and modes of feeling that sets the novel's moral tone.

memes.label: Character
memes.description: Character as the central observational category in Sterne — the study of human nature through faces, manners, and the traces life leaves on the soul.

memes.label: Charity
memes.description: The moral virtue at the heart of Yorick's sentimental encounters — the spontaneous impulse to give, which Sterne examines through comedy, theology, and social critique.

memes.label: Chevalier de St. Louis
memes.description: A former gallant officer reduced to selling pâtés on the streets of Versailles — Yorick hears his story of military service and poverty, and later learns the king granted him a pension in Sterne's A Sentimental Journey.

memes.label: Count de B—
memes.description: A nobleman at Versailles who grants Yorick a passport — a civilized, Shakespeare-loving French aristocrat who embodies enlightened French politeness in Sterne's novel.

memes.label: Courtesy
memes.description: Courtesy as social grace and moral signal in Sterne's novel — the codes of polite conduct that bridge strangers.

memes.label: Desire
memes.description: Desire as the propulsive force in Sterne's narrative — the longing that drives both travel and human connection, threading through every sentimental encounter.

memes.label: Dover
memes.description: The English Channel port from which Yorick embarks for France — the literal threshold of the sentimental journey and the place where the passport crisis first dawns.

memes.label: Duc de C—
memes.description: The French foreign minister whom Yorick must petition for a passport — an off-stage figure of political power in Sterne's A Sentimental Journey, based on the historical Duc de Choiseul.

memes.label: Eliza Draper
memes.description: Eliza Draper (1744–1778) was Laurence Sterne's real-life muse and romantic correspondent, the "pure taper" at which he relit his sentimental flame before embarking on his Journey — she is invoked at key moments throughout A Sentimental Journey as the absent beloved whose picture Yorick wears against his breast and to whom his most intimate apostrophes are addressed.

memes.label: Embarrassment
memes.description: Embarrassment as the signature emotional state in Sterne — the friction between social form and natural feeling that generates the novel's comic and sentimental energy.

memes.label: The Encounter with the Monk
memes.description: Yorick's famous encounter with the Franciscan monk in Calais — a pivotal sentimental episode.

memes.label: England
memes.description: Yorick's home country and the implicit cultural baseline against which all French manners are measured in Sterne's novel.

memes.label: Eugenius
memes.description: Yorick's dearest friend and confidant — the unnamed "Eugenius" to whom Yorick addresses his most intimate reflections throughout Sterne's A Sentimental Journey.

memes.label: Fancy
memes.description: Fancy as the mind's shaping power in Sterne's novel — the faculty that colours perception, populates empty spaces with images, and turns the ordinary into the sentimental.

memes.label: Feeling
memes.description: Feeling as the fundamental moral capacity in Sterne's sentimental universe — the pre-reflective responsiveness to others that precedes and grounds all refined sentiment, all moral judgment, and all human connection.

memes.label: Flanders
memes.description: The region between France and Holland — referenced in Sterne's novel as part of the alternative travel route home through Germany.

memes.label: Fortune
memes.description: Fortune as a narrative and philosophical force in Sterne's novel — the whimsical agency that brings strangers together.

memes.label: France
memes.description: The primary setting of Sterne's novel — an 18th-century kingdom Yorick traverses with a sentimental eye, contrasting French manners with English sensibilities.

memes.label: Friend
memes.description: Friendship as a model of sentimental relation in Sterne's novel — the voluntary bond that transcends rank, nation, and self-interest.

memes.label: Germany
memes.description: The German lands referenced twice in Sterne's novel — as the homeland of the dead ass mourner's three sons, and as a transit corridor on Yorick's proposed return route.

memes.label: Grace
memes.description: Grace as aesthetic quality and theological gift in Sterne's novel — the beauty that suffuses both conduct and creation.

memes.label: Happiness
memes.description: Happiness as the distant horizon of Sterne's sentimental journey — a state glimpsed, longed for, and philosophically interrogated rather than securely possessed.

memes.label: Heart
memes.description: The heart as the central sentimental organ in Sterne — the bodily seat of feeling, moral intuition, and authentic response that governs the journey.

memes.label: Heaven
memes.description: Heaven as divine agency, rhetorical appeal, and spiritual horizon in Sterne's novel — invoked by characters across all social stations as witness, resource, and ultimate destination.

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: Honour as a social and moral code in Sterne's novel — the tension between outward reputation and inward integrity.

memes.label: Hôtel de Modène
memes.description: Yorick's Parisian lodgings — an intimate interior where the crimson curtains frame encounters with the fille de chambre, La Fleur's loyalty, and the unfolding drama of the passport.

memes.label: Imagination
memes.description: Imagination as the transformative mental faculty in Sterne — the power that re-shapes perception, amplifies sentiment, and mediates between the world and the heart.

memes.label: in-the-desobligeant
memes.description: Yorick's philosophical meditation on human nature while seated in his desobligeant carriage in Calais, from Laurence Sterne's A Sentimental Journey (chapter IN THE DESOBLIGEANT).

memes.label: Italy
memes.description: The promised but unreached destination of Yorick's sentimental journey — a symbolic horizon that shapes the novel's structure without ever appearing in it.

memes.label: Janatone
memes.description: The landlord's daughter at Montreuil, briefly named in a scene that occasions Sterne's comic digression on French conversational hinges — tant pis and tant mieux.

memes.label: La Fleur
memes.description: Yorick's French servant, hired at Montreuil — a lively, resourceful, and devoted young man whose antics drive much of the novel's comedy and charm.

memes.label: Le Dimanche
memes.description: On a Sunday morning in Paris, La Fleur appears splendidly dressed and asks Yorick for the day off to court his petite demoiselle, prompting a digression on the vanities and natural rights of servants.

memes.label: Liberty
memes.description: Liberty as political ideal and personal condition in Sterne's novel — the starling's cry and the traveller's autonomy.

memes.label: Life
memes.description: Life as existential canvas in Sterne's novel — the span of mortal experience that sentimentalism seeks to fill with meaning and human connection.

memes.label: Lisle
memes.description: The Flemish border city — present-day Lille — mentioned in passing as one of the French captain's guesses about the lady's origin in the Calais remise scene.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The English capital and Yorick's home — the point of departure for his sentimental journey, referenced throughout the novel as a cultural counterpoint to Paris.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: Love as a central emotional theme in Sterne's novel — from romantic infatuation to universal benevolence.

memes.label: Lyons
memes.description: A French city on the route to Italy — referenced in Sterne's novel as the point where the Alps begin and rapid travel yields to caution.

memes.label: Madame de L—
memes.description: A gracious French lady whom Yorick encounters at Amiens and later visits in Paris — sister of Count de L—, woman of sentiment and social grace.

memes.label: Madame de R—
memes.description: An off-screen Parisian lady to whom Yorick carries a letter from Madame de L—; she lives in the Rue St. Pierre and her fille de chambre serves as intermediary, yet Yorick never actually meets her across the entire narrative.

memes.label: Madame de Rambouliet
memes.description: A virtuous Parisian lady of "the most correct" manners who takes Yorick in her coach and, with disarming candour, asks him to pull the cord — a brief encounter that embodies Sterne's fusion of politeness and natural bodily frankness.

memes.label: Madame de V—
memes.description: A Parisian woman "vibrating" between coquetry and deism whom Yorick claims to have "unperverted" — her religious debate with Yorick becomes a triumph of sentimental persuasion over Enlightenment scepticism in Sterne's novel.

memes.label: Maria of Moulines
memes.description: A grief-stricken young woman Yorick encounters at Moulines — her lost lover, dead father, and broken mind create one of the most famous sentimental tableaux in 18th-century literature.

memes.label: Marquis d'E—
memes.description: An impoverished Breton nobleman who surrenders his sword under an ancient law to pursue commerce in Martinico, then returns nineteen years later to reclaim it in a solemn ceremony witnessed by Yorick in Sterne's A Sentimental Journey.

memes.label: Meeting Maria
memes.description: The novel's most celebrated sentimental encounter — Yorick seeks out Maria, the grief-stricken girl from Tristram Shandy, near Moulines.

memes.label: Melancholy
memes.description: Melancholy as the characteristic eighteenth-century emotional tonality — sorrow refined into an aesthetic, a mood that Sterne's narrator cultivates as the proper attitude for sentimental perception.

memes.label: Mind
memes.description: The mind as the rational counterweight to the heart in Sterne — the faculty that reflects, judges, and sometimes obstructs the free flow of sentiment.

memes.label: Montreuil
memes.description: The French town where Yorick hires La Fleur as his servant — a pivotal stop on the road from Calais to Paris that introduces one of the novel's most beloved characters.

memes.label: Moulines
memes.description: The French town in the Bourbonnois where Yorick seeks out the disordered Maria — the setting for the novel's most celebrated scene of sentimental pathos.

memes.label: Mount Taurira
memes.description: A mountain on the route between Moulines and Lyons — the ascent where the postilion loses a shoe and the summit from which Yorick begins his descent toward Italy.

memes.label: Mundungus
memes.description: A wealthy but emotionally indifferent traveller in Sterne's A Sentimental Journey — paired with Smelfungus as a cautionary type of how fortune without sensibility yields no genuine experience.

memes.label: Nampont
memes.description: The post-house town on the road from Calais to Amiens where Yorick encounters the mourner of the dead ass — one of the most celebrated sentimental episodes in Sterne's novel.

memes.label: Naples
memes.description: The southern Italian city on the Grand Tour circuit — visited by Smelfungus on his joyless continental tour, a stop between Rome and Venice.

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: The 18th-century concept of Nature as both moral guide and human disposition, central to Sterne's sentimental philosophy.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The glittering capital where Yorick spends the longest portion of his journey — a city of spectacles, social theater, and cultural revelation.

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: Passion as the turbulent emotional force in Sterne's novel — both the threat to sentimental composure and the source of its deepest authenticity.

memes.label: Pity
memes.description: Pity as the central sentimental virtue in Sterne's novel — the tender emotion that dissolves the boundary between self and other.

memes.label: Pleasure
memes.description: Pleasure as sensory and sentimental experience in Sterne's novel — the immediate delight that opens the door to deeper feeling.

memes.label: Pont Neuf
memes.description: The oldest bridge in Paris — a site of chance encounter in Sterne's novel where Yorick's sentimental philosophy meets the urban crowd.

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: Pride as a complex moral sentiment in Sterne — both a satiric target and a natural human dignity that resists easy judgment.

memes.label: Quai de Conti
memes.description: The Parisian quay where Yorick purchases Shakespeare, walks with the fille de chambre, and first hears of the Count de B— — a setting that threads the novel's books, encounters, and aristocrats into one narrative knot.

memes.label: Reason
memes.description: Reason in Sterne's novel as the faculty that sentiment consistently overrules — necessary but insufficient, a "bubble" that music, feeling, and instinct dissolve.

memes.label: Religion
memes.description: Religion as institutional authority and personal sentiment in Sterne's novel — the monk, the dance, and the religion of the heart.

memes.label: Rennes
memes.description: The Breton capital where Yorick witnesses the Marquis d'E—'s solemn sword ceremony — one of the novel's most emotionally charged scenes and Sterne's meditation on nobility, commerce, and the recovery of honour.

memes.label: road-to-versailles
memes.description: Yorick's journey on the road from Paris to Versailles, interspersed with the story of a caged starling, from Laurence Sterne's A Sentimental Journey (chapter ROAD TO VERSAILLES).

memes.label: Rome
memes.description: The Eternal City on the Grand Tour route — referenced in Sterne's novel through the satirical figure of Smelfungus and as a symbol of classical pilgrimage.

memes.label: Rue de Gueneguault
memes.description: A Paris street near the Hotel de Modene — the setting for Yorick's tender farewell with the fille de chambre.

memes.label: Rue de Nevers
memes.description: A Paris street corner near the Pont Neuf — the setting for a parting glance in Sterne's novel, charged with unspoken sentiment.

memes.label: Savoy
memes.description: The rugged Alpine region between France and Italy — referenced in Sterne's novel as a landscape of hardship and the final barrier before the Italian states.

memes.label: Secret
memes.description: Secret as the currency of intimacy and social power in Sterne's novel — the knowledge held back that draws people together and the hidden springs of human conduct.

memes.label: Sentiment
memes.description: Sentiment as the core emotional currency of Sterne's novel — the refined feeling that distinguishes the sentimental traveller.

memes.label: Sentimentalism
memes.description: The 18th-century literary and philosophical movement that elevated emotion, sympathy, and moral feeling over pure reason — Sterne's novel is one of its defining works.

memes.label: Sigh
memes.description: The sigh as sentimental punctuation in Sterne's novel — a non-verbal sign of inward feeling that speaks where words fail.

memes.label: Silence
memes.description: Silence as communicative space in Sterne's novel — the charged intervals between words where sentiment travels most freely and intimacy is made or broken.

memes.label: Smelfungus
memes.description: A satirical caricature of Tobias Smollett — the splenetic traveller who finds fault with everything he encounters, from the Pantheon in Rome to the Venus de' Medici in Florence, serving as Sterne's foil for the ideal of sentimental travel.

memes.label: Sorrow
memes.description: Sorrow as the emotional landscape through which Yorick's sentimental journey must pass — the necessary counterweight that gives tenderness its gravity and meaning.

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: The soul as the seat of feeling, pity, and moral intuition in Sterne's sentimental vocabulary — where true character resides beyond social performance.

memes.label: Spain
memes.description: The Iberian kingdom referenced twice in Sterne's novel — as the origin of the dead ass mourner's pilgrimage and his destination of gratitude.

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: Spirit as the animating quality of character in Sterne's novel — the inner disposition revealed through conduct, social grace, and the capacity to be moved.

memes.label: Stranger
memes.description: The stranger as the central social category in Sterne — the unknown other whose encounter is the occasion for sentiment, courtesy, and the discovery of shared humanity.

memes.label: Tears
memes.description: Tears as the quintessential sentimental expression in Sterne's novel — the involuntary overflow of feeling that dissolves the boundary between self and other.

memes.label: Temper
memes.description: Temper as the quality of emotional self-governance in Sterne's novel — the internal balance that makes sentimental responsiveness possible without collapse into excess.

memes.label: The Act of Charity
memes.description: Yorick witnesses a beggar artfully soliciting twelve sous from two Parisian women in the dark passage behind the Opera Comique — a lesson in the mechanics of flattery and alms-giving.

memes.label: The Address
memes.description: En route to Versailles to petition the Duc de C— for a passport, Yorick debates how to address a great man without sacrificing dignity — arriving at a philosophy of equal encounter.

memes.label: The Barber
memes.description: A Parisian barber and periwig-maker whose grandiloquent language about the durability of a wig buckle — "you may emerge it into the ocean, and it will stand" — provokes Yorick's reflections on the French sublime and the difference between national characters.

memes.label: The Bastile
memes.description: The Paris prison-fortress whose threat haunts Yorick's stay in the capital — a symbol of French absolutism that he philosophically dismantles but never actually enters.

memes.label: The Beautiful Grisette
memes.description: The unnamed Parisian grisette who sells gloves to Yorick in a celebrated scene of silent erotic commerce — a shop-girl whose "quick black eye" and "long silken eyelashes" conduct a duel of glances across the counter, blending "whim, and sense, and seriousness, and nonsense" in a look that no language can express.

memes.label: The Bidet
memes.description: Yorick and La Fleur on the road from Montreuil — La Fleur's bidet refuses to pass a dead ass, throwing him twice, then bolts back to Montreuil, prompting Sterne's comic taxonomy of French exclamations (Diable! → Peste! → the ineffable third degree).

memes.label: The Bookseller
memes.description: A Parisian bookseller at the Quai de Conti who keeps a set of Shakespeare — a minor but pivotal figure whose shop becomes the setting for Yorick's encounter with the fille de chambre and the purchase that leads to his meeting with Count de B—.
aliases: the bookseller at the Quai de Conti

memes.label: The Bourbonnois
memes.description: A historic region in central France — "the sweetest part of France" — through which Yorick travels during the vintage season on his way to Italy; the landscape of abundance and rejoicing that forms the backdrop to his encounter with the disordered Maria at Moulines.

memes.label: The Captive
memes.description: Haunted by the caged starling's refrain "I can't get out," Yorick retreats to his room and conjures a devastating portrait of an imaginary prisoner — a fantasy of confinement so vivid it drives him to resolve immediately on petitioning the Duc de Choiseul for his passport.

memes.label: The Case of Conscience
memes.description: The hotel master confronts Yorick about a young woman in his room, launching a comedy of moral casuistry; a grisette selling lace tests Yorick's resolutions with patient sweetness.

memes.label: The Case of Delicacy
memes.description: The novel's final episode — stranded by a rockslide in Savoy, Yorick must share a bedchamber with a Piedmontese lady and her maid, leading to a delicately negotiated treaty of conduct.

memes.label: The Chevalier de St. Louis
memes.description: A decorated but impoverished French officer encountered by Yorick in Paris — the Chevalier de St. Louis, reduced to selling pâtés on the street after his regiment was disbanded, whose dignity in misfortune wins Yorick's pity and esteem, and whose story eventually reaches the king, earning him a pension of fifteen hundred livres a year.

memes.label: The Coach-yard
memes.description: The Calais coach-yard where Yorick surveys the vehicles and selects the desobligeant — opening scene of the novel's travel philosophy.

memes.label: The Conquest
memes.description: After the temptation scene with the fille de chambre, Yorick achieves a moral victory — locking his door, kissing her cheek, and leading her safely to the hotel gate, having governed his passions as a good man should.

memes.label: The Dead Ass
memes.description: Yorick's encounter with a grieving peasant mourning his dead donkey — one of the most celebrated sentimental episodes in the novel.

memes.label: The Desobligeant
memes.description: Yorick sits in his désobligeante at Calais and delivers one of the novel's most famous meta-fictional meditations — on travel, discontent, "sentimental commerce," and the carriage itself as a metaphor for the digressive narrative to come.

memes.label: The Dwarf
memes.description: A dwarfish man at the Paris Opéra Comique who struggles to see the performance, becomes the victim of a tall German's indifference, and is finally helped by a French officer's intervention — a vignette of sentimental justice in Sterne's novel.

memes.label: The Fille de Chambre
memes.description: A young lady's maid in Paris — employed by Madame de R—, she sells lace to Yorick, walks with him along the Quai de Conti, and becomes the subject of one of the novel's most erotically charged episodes.

memes.label: The Fragment
memes.description: Sterne's meta-fictional fragment — the story of Abdera, where a performance of Euripides' Andromeda so moves the entire city that "the golden age returned" — a parable about the civilising power of art embedded within the narrative of A Sentimental Journey.

memes.label: The Gloves
memes.description: Yorick visits a Paris glove shop and engages in a silent, erotically charged exchange of glances with the beautiful grisette — one of Sterne's finest studies in wordless seduction, commerce, and the rhetoric of the unsaid.

memes.label: The Grace
memes.description: After the humble supper in Savoy, the old peasant calls his family to dance — a nightly ritual of joyous thanksgiving in which Yorick perceives "Religion mixing in the dance," the peasant's grace more eloquent than any prelate's sermon.

memes.label: The Grisset
memes.description: A young Parisian shop-girl who sells Yorick gloves — a fleeting but vivid encounter that exemplifies the charged, wordless gallantry of Sterne's sentimental mode.

memes.label: the-hotel-at-paris
memes.description: Yorick's arrival at the Hotel de Modene in Paris and his comic struggle with asking directions in broken French, recounted in chapter THE HOTEL AT PARIS from Laurence Sterne's A Sentimental Journey.

memes.label: The Husband
memes.description: The Parisian shopkeeper who emerges from a back parlour in his nightcap while Yorick feels his wife's pulse — Sterne's satirical portrait of the emasculated husband in Parisian commerce.

memes.label: The Landlord
memes.description: The innkeeper at Montreuil who introduces La Fleur to Yorick — a shrewd, good-natured French provincial who embodies the commercial warmth of French hospitality.

memes.label: The Letter
memes.description: La Fleur's first service for Yorick — delivering the Count de L—'s letter, fabricating a return billet to Madame de L—, and providing a drummer's love letter as epistolary template — a comedy of servant zeal staged in Amiens.

memes.label: The Monk
memes.description: A poor Franciscan monk who begs alms from Yorick at Calais — Yorick's refusal and subsequent remorse launches the novel's central meditation on charity and sentiment.

memes.label: The Mystery
memes.description: Standing at the gate of the Hôtel de Modène, Yorick observes a mysterious beggar who solicits exclusively from women — whispering secrets with invariable success — and is baffled by an unsolved riddle.

memes.label: The Notary
memes.description: A Parisian notary whose windy misadventures are recounted in a fragment Yorick attempts to read — a comic figure of professional dignity undone by domestic and meteorological forces.

memes.label: The Old French Officer
memes.description: A retired French military officer Yorick meets in Paris — a man of quiet dignity, reading a pamphlet, who becomes an unexpected source of wisdom about honour and national character.

memes.label: The Opera
memes.description: The Paris opera house where Yorick encounters the old French officer and the dwarf — a theater of sentimental sociability and the site of one of Sterne's most famous scenes of human sympathy.

memes.label: The Passport
memes.description: Yorick's passport crisis — from the Dover realisation that England was at war with France, through the Bastile threat in Paris, to the comic resolution at Versailles where Shakespeare and the "king's jester" secure his freedom — one of the longest narrative arcs in Sterne's A Sentimental Journey, spanning six chapters and involving La Fleur, Count de B—, the Duc de Choiseul, the starling, and the identity of Yorick himself.

memes.label: The Postilion
memes.description: The carriage driver who conveys Yorick through northern France — remembered for his galloping insensitivity after the Dead Ass episode and his practical skill on the road to Italy.

memes.label: The Pulse
memes.description: Yorick asks a beautiful Paris grisette for directions to the Opéra Comique, and the encounter escalates into a mock-medical flirtation as he takes her pulse in her shop.

memes.label: The Remise
memes.description: The coach-house in Calais where Yorick encounters the mysterious Flemish lady — the setting for the novel's first sustained sentimental drama.

memes.label: The Remise Door
memes.description: The coach-house doorway at Calais — a threshold where Yorick pauses to meditate on captivity, freedom, and the starling's cry.

memes.label: The Riddle
memes.description: Yorick observes a mysterious beggar outside his Paris hotel — a man who asks charity only from women, whispers a secret in each ear, and is never refused — a riddle that occupies Yorick's mind as one of the novel's teasing unsolved mysteries.

memes.label: The Riddle Explained
memes.description: The resolution of the riddle that puzzled Yorick outside his Paris hotel — following the mysterious beggar, he discovers that the secret whispered in women's ears is flattery, "delicious essence" that sweetly mixes with the blood and helps it through "the most difficult and tortuous passages to the heart."

memes.label: The Rose
memes.description: At the Paris opera, the old French officer teaches Yorick about national character and toleration, followed by the scandalously delicate story of Madame de Rambouliet's coach ride.

memes.label: The Snuff Box
memes.description: Yorick's reconciliation with the Franciscan monk at Calais through the exchange of snuff boxes — the monk's horn box becomes a portable shrine of sentiment that Yorick guards "as I would the instrumental parts of my religion," and upon the monk's death, the box provokes a flood of tears at his grave.

memes.label: The Starling
memes.description: A caged bird that repeats the phrase "I can't get out" — the most famous symbolic figure in Sterne's novel, adopted by Yorick as his coat of arms and emblem of the universal longing for liberty.

memes.label: The Supper
memes.description: A quietly luminous domestic scene near the novel's close — Yorick shares a humble supper with a French peasant family, discovering grace in simplicity.

memes.label: The Sword
memes.description: At Rennes, the ruined Marquis d'E— surrenders his ancestral sword under an ancient Breton law, rebuilds his fortune over nineteen years in Martinico, and returns to reclaim the blade in a solemn ceremony that moves Yorick to tears — "O, how I envied him his feelings!"

memes.label: The Temptation
memes.description: At sunset in his Paris hotel room, Yorick faces a powerful temptation when the beautiful fille de chambre visits — an episode of moral struggle, blushes, and hard-won victory.

memes.label: The Translation
memes.description: Yorick attends the Paris opera where the old French officer's silent gesture of removing his spectacles inspires a meditation on translating looks and limbs into words.

memes.label: Travel
memes.description: The organizing metaphor of Sterne's novel — travel as both physical movement through 18th-century France and an inward sentimental journey of moral discovery.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: Truth in Sterne's novel as a layered concept — from divine "eternal truth" to the social truth that is always partial, negotiated, and qualified by sentiment.

memes.label: Turin
memes.description: The Italian city that is Yorick's stated destination — the promised "Italy" of the novel's title that remains perpetually on the horizon and is never actually reached.

memes.label: Venice
memes.description: Venice appears in Sterne's novel through Smelfungus's satirical itinerary — a stop on the Grand Tour rendered as a litany of complaints.

memes.label: Versailles
memes.description: The royal palace town outside Paris where Yorick seeks a passport from the Duc de C— — a stage for encounters with court power, the Chevalier de St. Louis, and the Count de B—.

memes.label: Virtue
memes.description: Virtue as moral ideal and practical test in Sterne's novel — the sentimental tension between professed principle and felt impulse.

memes.label: Yorick
memes.description: The narrator and protagonist of A Sentimental Journey — an English clergyman whose acute sensibility, self-deprecating wit, and moral curiosity define the novel's sentimental vision.
